key_insight: Phosphorus-doped triangulene can be assembled into superatomic graphene with tunable flat bands and intrinsic half-metallic magnetism, enabled by in-plane p_x,y frontier orbitals.
engineering_relevance: This bottom-up surface-synthesis strategy offers a design route to engineer correlated 2D quantum materials with controllable electronic and magnetic properties.
method: On-surface synthesis of phosphorus-doped triangulene building blocks to form a honeycomb superatomic lattice
Scanning tunneling microscopy/spectroscopy to verify lattice order and map Dirac/flat-band states
Density functional theory to identify orbital origin of flat bands and predict magnetic behavior
evidence_signal: STM/STS directly resolved the honeycomb superatomic graphene and its Dirac-band plus flat-band signatures, while DFT linked these features to the molecular frontier orbitals.
watch_item: The results rely on precise molecular synthesis and surface assembly, so scalability and robustness beyond the prepared platform may be challenging.
paper_title: Tunable Flat Bands and magnetism in Triangulene-based Superatomic Graphene
